figures.caption: Figure 6: Forward qualitative examples on REC/CRR: CoRDS (ours) accumulates the three javelin-throw occurrences and tracks pre-/post-clue answers across time, while InfiniPot-V undercounts and answers affirmatively before the clue.
Table 13: Full layer-scope sweep. Time is normalized to the fastest setting.
Table 1: Offline video understanding benchmarks. Green numbers indicate the absolute improvement over the closest compressed competitor under the same backbone and cache budget.

Table 14: Cascade-layer ablation on Video-MME (n=500) and MLVUdev (n=200) using Qwen2-VL-7BInstruct. Anchor layers run the full selector, follower layers reuse anchor selections, and highlighted rows mark the recommended accuracy/latency trade-off.
Figure 5: Backward qualitative examples on EPM/ASI: CoRDS (ours) recovers the clue laid down hundreds of seconds before the question, while InfiniPot-V drops it.

Figure 1: CoRDS framework. As streaming frames arrive, the accumulated KV cache is compressed by D2-style coreset selection in joint K-V space (Eq. 4) with an orthogonal anti-redundancy term (Eq. 9); the persistent cache is then used to answer asynchronous question queries.
Table 2: Integrated comparison on OVO-Bench and StreamingBench using Qwen2-VL-7B under a 6K compression budget. Qwen2-VL-7B aggregates are taken from the OVO-Bench[19].

Figure 8: Coverage CDF of nearest-retained cosine distance for eight MLVU tasks under three KV-compression settings (Joint K∥V, K only, V only). Each main panel shows the full CDF; the inset in the lower-right magnifies the region around the maximum gap, with the marker line and annotation reporting max∆CDF = CDFours − CDFInfiniPot and the distance d at which it occurs. Higher CDF at smaller d indicates better coverage of the original token set by the retained tokens. 19

Table 5: Offline long video understanding evaluation results under memory-constrained scenario (case 3), with MC (Memory-Constrained) and QA (Query-Agnostic) conditions marked. Results are reported on (1) Video-MME – Short: -3min, Medium: 3-30min, Long: 30min-2h, and (2) MLVU – Holistic, Single-Detail, Multi-Detail LVU.
Figure 4: Coverage CDF on MLVU anomaly recognition (Qwen2-VL-7B, |M |=6K). Curves further left indicate better full-cache coverage under joint K∥V , K-only, and V -only metrics.

Table 10: Effect of the follower module across all Ours variants. Bold marks the better of {base, + follower} within each variant pair. The all-layer variant (no follower) is shown for reference. The follower module yields a larger relative gain on the lighter bottom-25 backbone than on bottom-50, and its memory benefit grows monotonically with L. At L = 1000 s the source video yields ≈ 864 effective frames.

Figure 7: Real-time qualitative examples on STU/ATR/ACR/OCR: with the clue at the ask frame, CoRDS (ours) reads fine-grained on-screen content and short-horizon actions where InfiniPot-V picks plausible distractors.
